source: Sed, Awk & Grep - Awk command: What awk is, its record/field model, and program structure

questions.q: Where does the name 'awk' come from?
options: The initials of its creators: Aho, Weinberger, Kernighan (Bell Labs, 1977)
questions.why: awk is named for Alfred Aho, Peter Weinberger and Brian Kernighan.

questions.q: On Rocky Linux, which implementation is awk?
options: gawk (GNU awk) -- /usr/bin/awk is a symlink to gawk
questions.why: Rocky's awk points to gawk; Debian/Ubuntu often default to mawk.

questions.q: In awk's model, each line is a ___ and each part of it is a ___.
questions.why: awk processes records (rows) made of fields (columns), like a relational database.

questions.q: By default, what separates fields in awk?
questions.why: The default field separator FS is whitespace (spaces/tabs).

questions.q: What is the difference between $0 and $1?
options: $0 is the entire line/record; $1 is the first field
$0 is the first field; $1 is the line
$0 is the line count; $1 is the filename

questions.q: What is the general shape of an awk invocation?
questions.why: The pattern selects records and the {action} runs on them; either part can be omitted.

options: Read the program from a file
questions.why: -F sets FS; -f reads a program file; -v sets a variable.

questions.q: When do BEGIN{} and END{} blocks run?
options: BEGIN runs before any records are read; END runs after all records are read
questions.why: BEGIN and END bracket the per-record main loop.
